title: paralyxia macrophylla
side_effects: Paralyxia macrophylla can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea and vomiting due to its emetic properties, which can lead to dehydration if not managed correctly. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also cause cardiac arrhythmias and hypotension, potentially leading to fainting or even heart failure in severe cases. Additionally, improper use of paralyxia macrophylla may result in liver and kidney damage due to its toxic compounds, which can be exacerbated by pre-existing conditions. It is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any plant-based remedy to avoid these potential side effects.
